skill.name: dice-easy-apply
skill.description: Automate Dice.com Easy Apply searches with guarded Puppeteer workflows, resume uploads, remote-role filters, and application tracking.
skill.summary: Guides guarded Dice.com Easy Apply automation for remote software roles.
skill.author: ralyodio

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer over-reported Markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution, and those specific findings are false positives. The skill still has medium operational risk because it directs agents to browse Dice.com with Puppeteer, reuse a persistent authenticated Chromium profile, upload personal resume files, write state logs, and launch Chromium with sandbox-disabling flags.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Job Application Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use Dice Easy Apply, upload a verified resume and cover letter, continue to review, and submit applications. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it can disclose personal information or submit applications without sufficient human review if operators do not keep the guardrails.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly describe Easy Apply submission and document upload. The risk is operational and privacy-related rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Chromium Sandbox Disabled
medium_findings.description: The Puppeteer launch arguments include --no-sandbox and --disable-setuid-sandbox while browsing third-party web pages. These flags reduce Chromium process isolation and increase impact if a visited page exploits the browser process.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sandbox-disabling flags are directly visible in the launch example. They are common in containers, but still materially reduce browser isolation.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Profile and State Files Retain Sensitive Context
medium_findings.description: The workflow recommends a persistent Chromium profile plus state and log files under a temporary directory. These files may retain login session artifacts, application history, job URLs, and document-use metadata if permissions and cleanup are not managed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly recommends a persistent browser profile and local state/log paths. The content also warns against credential storage, so this is a cautionary risk, not evidence of theft.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and inline code spans as Ruby shell backtick execution. The reviewed file contains documentation examples, URLs, JavaScript, bash snippets, and inline labels; no Ruby backtick execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fences or inline code examples, not executable Ruby source. This directly explains the bulk of the external_commands static findings.

low_findings.title: Expected Dice.com Network Targets
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Dice.com search and job-application pages that match the skill purpose. No evidence found of unrelated endpoints, webhook delivery, encoded payload exfiltration, or covert network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains and paths are consistent with Dice job search and application flows. This supports dismissing the hardcoded URL findings as expected network use.

low_findings.title: Environment Variables Used for Local Configuration
low_findings.description: The environment variable examples configure document paths, browser binary path, profile path, search limits, and dry-run settings. No evidence found that environment variables are sent to an external service or used to collect secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access appears limited to local configuration for Puppeteer and file selection. The same file explicitly says credentials should not be stored in scripts or logs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Automation Against Authenticated Third-Party Site
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines Puppeteer browsing, a persistent login profile, and automated application submission on Dice.com. This pattern should require human review for CAPTCHA, MFA, identity prompts, and final submission decisions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly uses authenticated browser automation and application submission. The file also includes guardrails, so the pattern is risky but not malicious by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Personal Document Upload Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the agent to verify and upload a resume PDF and optional cover-letter PDF. These files can contain personal contact, employment, and identity information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The resume and cover letter upload behavior is directly documented. The privacy risk is inherent to the stated feature.

content.value_statement: Manual Dice.com applications are repetitive and easy to duplicate. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through guarded searches, uploads, skips, and application logs.
actual_capabilities: Builds Dice.com search URLs with remote and Easy Apply filters.
Defines search terms for software, AI, full-stack, frontend, and backend roles.
Provides Puppeteer launch guidance for a persistent Chromium profile.
Checks local resume and cover-letter PDF paths before upload.
Defines skip rules for onsite, hybrid, travel, clearance, and unknown required questions.
Specifies state and results files to avoid duplicate applications.
limitations: Requires the user to provide valid Dice access and local document paths.
Does not solve CAPTCHA, MFA, suspicious login, or identity verification prompts.
Does not answer custom employer questions or fabricate applicant details.
Depends on Dice page structure and may need updates when the site changes.

use_cases.title: Run a guarded daily search
use_cases.description: Search remote Dice Easy Apply roles and keep a local record of seen, skipped, and applied jobs.

use_cases.title: Review role fit before applying
use_cases.description: Inspect each job description for remote status, role match, and disqualifying constraints before submission.

use_cases.title: Prototype application automation
use_cases.description: Use the documented Puppeteer flow as a starting point for a controlled job-board automation script.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a dry run
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dice-easy-apply skill to prepare a dry run for remote React and full-stack roles. Verify my resume and cover-letter paths before opening Dice.

prompt_templates.title: Search and classify roles
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to search Dice Easy Apply remote roles for my target queries. Classify each role as apply, skip, or needs review with a short reason.

prompt_templates.title: Apply with guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Dice Easy Apply workflow with MAX_APPLY set to five. Stop before any unknown required question, CAPTCHA, MFA, or external ATS flow.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a previous run
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill state and results files to summarize submitted, skipped, duplicate, and failed Dice applications. Identify any role that needs manual follow-up.

output_examples.input: Search remote Gen AI and full-stack Dice Easy Apply roles in dry-run mode.
output_examples.output: A concise run summary with searched queries, candidate roles, skip reasons, and applications that would be submitted after review.

output_examples.input: Apply to approved roles only and stop for custom questions.
output_examples.output: A supervised application report listing submitted jobs, skipped jobs, unknown questions, and the saved state and log locations.
A readable audit summary showing submitted roles, duplicate detections, failed uploads, and recommended manual follow-up items.

best_practices: Run with dry-run enabled before any live submissions.
Use a dedicated browser profile and review every final submission screen.
Clean up state files and profile data when the campaign is complete.
anti_patterns: Do not store Dice credentials in scripts, prompts, logs, or skill files.
Do not bypass CAPTCHA, MFA, identity checks, or account-security prompts.
Do not submit applications when required questions are unknown or unverifiable.

faq.question: Does this skill include Dice credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that credentials should not be stored in scripts, memory, logs, or reports.

faq.question: Can it submit applications automatically?
faq.answer: It describes a supervised Easy Apply workflow. Users should review each final screen before submission.

faq.question: Does it support non-remote roles?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow focuses on Dice remote workplace filters and skips onsite or hybrid constraints.

faq.question: What files does it need?
faq.answer: It expects local resume and optional cover-letter PDF paths provided by the user.

faq.question: Will it handle CAPTCHA or MFA?
faq.answer: No. The safety rules require stopping for CAPTCHA, MFA, identity verification, or suspicious-login checks.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill handles personal documents, browser sessions, third-party web automation, and local logs, even though no malicious endpoint was found.
